health.mental_health: Late-onset depression with periods of low motivation and sleep disruption, worsened by stress from re-entering school and trying to return to paid work; engaged in therapy and tracking mood/energy to avoid slipping during the dark winter months.
recent_visits: Urology follow-up for urinary symptoms and PSA monitoring (Alaska Urology, midtown Anchorage)
Primary care visit for blood pressure medication adjustment and labs (Providence Primary Care)
Behavioral health counseling session focused on depression management and life-transition stress (telehealth)
Fasting lipid and A1C bloodwork at an outpatient lab

finance.income_range: $25,000–$45,000/year household take-home (part-time work plus spouse/partner income varies)
finance.debt: Car loan with about 2 years remaining and a small-but-growing balance on a credit card used for tuition/books; actively considering federal student aid and a payment plan for community college fees.
investments: Employer 401(k) from prior job (no longer contributing regularly)
Alaska 529 plan for a child’s remaining education expenses
recent_transactions: Tuition payment plan installment to University of Alaska Anchorage
Used bookstore purchase for course materials and access code
Pharmacy co-pays for blood pressure and prostate medications
Vehicle maintenance (winter tires swap and alignment in Anchorage)
Payment to a licensed counselor (copay via insurance)

relationships: Spouse/partner: Ingrid Larsson (primary household co-planner and main wage earner most months)
Child: Noah Larsson (early 20s, mostly independent but occasionally needs support)
Child: Leila Larsson (teen, lives at home; Khalil handles most weekday logistics)
Older sibling: Samir Larsson (lives Outside; periodic check-ins about family responsibilities)
Close friend: Dan Erickson (neighbor in Anchorage who helps with rides during heavy snow)
Study group contact: Marisol Vega (mature-student peer at UAA)
social.social_media_activity: Low-volume Facebook use (community groups, school announcements, local events) and occasional Nextdoor posts about neighborhood safety and snow/road conditions; avoids posting about health and finances.
private_conversations: Discussing urinary symptoms and intimacy strain related to prostate issues
Talking with spouse about depressive episodes and fear of not keeping up in classes
Negotiating boundaries with family about childcare and household workload while he studies
Confiding in a friend about worries over blood pressure and long-term health

schedule.work_hours: Weekdays 9:30am–2:30pm part-time (seasonal/variable) plus stay-at-home parenting duties before/after school; evenings reserved for coursework.
regular_appointments: Weekly counseling session (telehealth) on Tuesday evenings
Blood pressure self-check twice weekly at home; monthly nurse/clinic BP check when readings run high
Urology follow-up every 6 months (more often if symptoms flare)
Evening class meetings twice a week during the semester (UAA)
upcoming_events: Midterm exam week and group presentation for an intro business/office tech course
Follow-up with primary care to review labs (A1C, lipids) and medication tolerance
Career services appointment at UAA to review resume and practice interviews
schedule.daily_routine: Up early to get the teen out the door, handles errands and household tasks mid-morning, works a short shift when scheduled, then cooks dinner and studies after 8pm; tries to fit in a brisk indoor walk or mall laps during icy weeks.

professional.company: Part-time front desk/administrative support through a local staffing agency serving medical and nonprofit offices in Anchorage
projects: Building a modern resume and LinkedIn profile tailored to administrative roles
Completing a UAA certificate track (office administration/business fundamentals) while working
Practicing Excel, scheduling software, and basic bookkeeping for job readiness
Updating references from prior employment and volunteer roles
professional.performance_issues: Occasional concentration dips and fatigue tied to depression and disrupted sleep; has requested predictable shifts to accommodate classes and medical appointments, and worries this makes him less competitive for full-time roles.
workplace_relationships: Supportive onsite supervisor who prefers clear availability and punctuality
A younger coworker who sometimes questions his tech skills, motivating him to over-prepare
Career counselor at UAA helping him translate caregiving years into workplace skills

hobbies: Salmon fishing on the Kenai when time and weather allow
Reading history and memoirs (often audiobooks while doing chores)
Aurora watching and night photography in winter (short outings)
Walking at the Dimond Center or indoor tracks during icy conditions
habits: Tracks blood pressure and sleep in a notebook to share with his doctor
Limits caffeine after noon to reduce nighttime bathroom trips
Uses a pill organizer to avoid missed doses during busy school weeks
Occasional evening alcohol (1–2 drinks) but tries to avoid it when mood is low
Winter light therapy lamp use to help with seasonal mood dips
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives in a modest single-family home in Anchorage with spouse/partner and teenage child; manages most cooking, errands, and winter prep while balancing part-time work and returning to school.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Working toward a heart-healthy, lower-sodium diet (more fish, oats, and vegetables); tries to reduce processed foods but relies on quick meals during exam weeks.
